indication: Melanoma — intralesional injection into cutaneous, subcutaneous and/or nodal lesions that are visible, palpable or detectable by ultrasound guidance. Initial (first) administration only.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Single administration — first dose only at 10⁶ PFU/ml
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Intratumoral injection. First administration only at 10⁶ PFU/ml concentration. Total injection volume must not exceed 4ml per treatment session. Inject the largest lesion first; prioritise remaining lesions by size. Volume per lesion: >5cm up to 4ml; 2.5-5cm up to 2ml; 1.5-2.5cm up to 1ml; 0.5-1.5cm up to 0.5ml; ≤0.5cm up to 0.1ml. Light-sensitive genetically modified product — do not remove from manufacturer's packaging. Store frozen; record time removed from freezer and time dispensed.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No specific haematological delay criteria stated for first administration. Second dose given 21 days after first; subsequent doses every 14 days at 10⁸ PFU/ml (see separate protocol).

monitoring_requirements: Patient Information Card to be confirmed (yes/no) prior to administration
Record time removed from freezer, time dispensed, time put in pharmacy fridge
special_warnings: Genetically modified product — handle with appropriate biosafety precautions
Light-sensitive product — do not remove from manufacturer's packaging until required
This prescription is for the FIRST administration only at 10⁶ PFU/ml; second and subsequent doses use 10⁸ PFU/ml (separate protocol)
No dose reductions specified — volume adjusted by lesion size
Talimogene laherparepvec: initial dose day 1, second dose day 22, then every 14 days. Biohazard precautions required for handling — live attenuated HSV-1.

required_patient_fields.lesion_sizes_cm: Required to calculate volume per lesion

drugs.frequency: Single administration — first dose only at 10⁶ PFU/ml
drugs.special_instructions: Intratumoral injection. First administration only at 10⁶ PFU/ml concentration. Total injection volume must not exceed 4ml per treatment session. Inject the largest lesion first; prioritise remaining lesions by size. Volume per lesion: >5cm up to 4ml; 2.5-5cm up to 2ml; 1.5-2.5cm up to 1ml; 0.5-1.5cm up to 0.5ml; ≤0.5cm up to 0.1ml. Light-sensitive genetically modified product — do not remove from manufacturer's packaging. Store frozen; record time removed from freezer and time dispensed.

Volume per lesion: >5cm up to 4ml; 2.5-5cm up to 2ml; 1.5-2.5cm up to 1ml; 0.5-1.5cm up to 0.5ml; ≤0.5cm up to 0.1ml. Light-sensitive genetically modified product — do not remove from manufacturer's packaging. Store frozen; record time removed from freezer and time dispensed.

indication: Melanoma — intralesional injection into cutaneous, subcutaneous and/or nodal lesions that are visible, palpable or detectable by ultrasound guidance. Second and subsequent administrations only.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Second dose 21 days after first dose; thereafter every 14 days
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Intratumoral injection. Second and subsequent administrations at 10⁸ PFU/ml concentration. Total injection volume must not exceed 4ml per treatment session. Inject any new lesions first; then prioritise remaining lesions by size. Volume per lesion: >5cm up to 4ml; 2.5-5cm up to 2ml; 1.5-2.5cm up to 1ml; 0.5-1.5cm up to 0.5ml; ≤0.5cm up to 0.1ml. Light-sensitive genetically modified product — do not remove from manufacturer's packaging. Store frozen; record time removed from freezer and time dispensed.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Second dose given 21 days after the first treatment. Thereafter treatments may be scheduled every 14 days.

monitoring_requirements: Patient Information Card to be confirmed (yes/no) prior to each administration
Record time removed from freezer, time dispensed, time put in pharmacy fridge
special_warnings: Genetically modified product — handle with appropriate biosafety precautions
Light-sensitive product — do not remove from manufacturer's packaging until required
This prescription is for the SECOND AND SUBSEQUENT administrations only at 10⁸ PFU/ml; first dose uses 10⁶ PFU/ml (separate protocol)
No dose reductions specified — volume adjusted by lesion size

required_patient_fields.lesion_sizes_cm: Required to calculate volume per lesion

drugs.frequency: Second dose 21 days after first dose; thereafter every 14 days
drugs.special_instructions: Intratumoral injection. Second and subsequent administrations at 10⁸ PFU/ml concentration. Total injection volume must not exceed 4ml per treatment session. Inject any new lesions first; then prioritise remaining lesions by size. Volume per lesion: >5cm up to 4ml; 2.5-5cm up to 2ml; 1.5-2.5cm up to 1ml; 0.5-1.5cm up to 0.5ml; ≤0.5cm up to 0.1ml. Total injection volume must not exceed 4ml per treatment session. Inject any new lesions first; then prioritise remaining lesions by size. Volume per lesion: >5cm up to 4ml; 2.5-5cm up to 2ml; 1.5-2.5cm up to 1ml; 0.5-1.5cm up to 0.5ml; ≤0.5cm up to 0.1ml. indication: Unresectable or metastatic BRAF V600 mutation-positive melanoma where the following conditions apply: confirmed histological diagnosis of malignant melanoma which is BRAF V600 mutation positive; unresectable stage III or stage IV disease staged according to the AJCC 8th edition; treatment naive to BRAF V600 and MEK inhibitors for malignant melanoma unless previously received adjuvant dabrafenib and trametinib without progression, or dabrafenib plus trametinib for advanced disease stopped within 3 months solely due to dose-limiting toxicity in clear absence of progression; sufficient WHO performance status; treatment continued until loss of clinical benefit, unacceptable toxicity, or withdrawal of consent; formal medical review at least by end of first 8 weeks; no treatment breaks >6 weeks beyond expected 4-weekly cycle length.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Available as 15mg tablets. Swallow whole with water with or without food. If a dose is missed, do not take if less than 6 hours until next dose. In case of vomiting, do not re-take dose — take next scheduled dose. Dose reduction ladder: Level 0 (starting) 45mg BD; Level -1: 30mg BD; Level -2: Not recommended (permanently discontinue).

Available as 75mg and 50mg capsules. Swallow whole with water with or without food. If a dose is missed, take only if more than 12 hours until next scheduled dose. In case of vomiting, do not re-take dose. Dose reduction ladder: Level 0 (starting) 450mg OD; Level -1: 300mg OD; Level -2: 200mg OD; Level -3: 100mg OD (limited data; permanently discontinue if unable to tolerate 100mg OD). If binimetinib is temporarily interrupted, reduce encorafenib to 300mg OD during interruption (450mg as monotherapy not well tolerated). Mild hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh A): reduce to 300mg OD. Not recommended in severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C). Use with caution in severe renal impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Treatment-related toxicities (both drugs simultaneously unless specific exception applies)
dose_modifications.notes: If treatment-related toxicities occur, both binimetinib AND encorafenib should be simultaneously interrupted, dose reduced, or discontinued. Exceptions where only binimetinib modified: LVEF reduction, RPED/RVO, ILD/pneumonitis, CK elevation/rhabdomyolysis, VTE. Exceptions where only encorafenib modified: PPE, uveitis, QTc prolongation.

Maintain both agents. If no improvement within 2 weeks, withhold binimetinib until grade 0-1 or baseline, then resume same dose. If no improvement within further 2 weeks (4 weeks total), withhold encorafenib until grade 0-1 or baseline, then resume same dose.

dose_modifications.condition: AST/ALT First occurrence Grade 3 AND bilirubin >2x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold both agents for up to 4 weeks. If improved to grade 0-1 or baseline, resume both at reduced dose. If no improvement, permanently discontinue both.
Withhold both for up to 4 weeks. If improved to grade 0-1 or baseline, resume at reduced dose. If no improvement, permanently discontinue both.
Consider permanent discontinuation of both agents.
Both agents permanently discontinued.

dose_modifications.condition: Asymptomatic NCI-CTC Grade 2 LVEF decrease (absolute >10% from baseline AND below LLN)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold binimetinib for up to 4 weeks; temporarily reduce encorafenib dose. Resume binimetinib at reduced dose if within 4 weeks: LVEF at or above LLN AND absolute decrease from baseline is 10% or less. If LVEF does not recover within 4 weeks, permanently discontinue binimetinib (and encorafenib).

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 3 or 4 LVEF decrease OR symptomatic LVD
dose_modifications.notes: Binimetinib (and encorafenib) permanently discontinued.
Withhold encorafenib (binimetinib may be continued). Once QTc ≤500ms, restart encorafenib at reduced dose.
Permanently stop encorafenib (and binimetinib).

Withhold binimetinib for up to 4 weeks (temporarily reduce encorafenib). If improved to grade 0-1, resume binimetinib at reduced dose. If not resolved within 4 weeks, permanently discontinue binimetinib (and encorafenib).
Permanently discontinue binimetinib (and encorafenib).

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 1-2 uveitis not responding to specific ocular therapy, or Grade 3 uveitis
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt encorafenib and binimetinib; repeat ophthalmic monitoring within 2 weeks. If grade 1 and improves to grade 0, resume at same dose. If grade 2-3 and improves to grade 0-1, resume at reduced encorafenib dose. If not improved within 6 weeks, permanently discontinue encorafenib and binimetinib.

Interrupt binimetinib (dose reduce encorafenib for period of interruption only) for up to 2 weeks; repeat ophthalmic monitoring. If improved to grade 0-1, resume binimetinib at same dose. If improved to grade 2, resume at lower dose. If not improved to grade 2, permanently discontinue treatment.
Permanently discontinue binimetinib (and also encorafenib).
Maintain binimetinib dose; ensure adequate hydration.
Withhold binimetinib (temporarily reduce encorafenib) until improved to grade 0-1; ensure adequate hydration.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 3 or 4 (CK >5x ULN) with muscle symptoms or renal impairment
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold binimetinib (temporarily reduce encorafenib) until NCI-CTC grade 0-1. If resolved within 4 weeks, resume binimetinib at reduced dose. If not resolved, permanently discontinue binimetinib (and encorafenib).

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 2 rash (worsening or not improving within 2 weeks)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold binimetinib until improved to grade 0-1; resume at same dose (first occurrence) or reduced dose (recurrent grade 2).
Withhold both until improved to grade 0-1; resume at same dose (first occurrence) or reduced dose (recurrent grade 3).
Both permanently discontinued.

Maintain encorafenib and institute supportive measures (topical therapy). If not improved despite supportive therapy within 2 weeks, withhold encorafenib (binimetinib may continue) until grade 0-1; resume at same or reduced dose.
Withhold encorafenib (binimetinib may continue); institute supportive measures. Resume encorafenib at same or reduced dose when improved to grade 0-1.
Withhold binimetinib (encorafenib may continue). If improved to grade 0-1, resume binimetinib at reduced dose. If not improved, permanently discontinue binimetinib (and encorafenib).

dose_modifications.condition: Recurrent/intolerable grade 2 OR first occurrence grade 3 adverse reactions
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold both for up to 4 weeks. If improved to grade 0-1 or baseline, resume at reduced dose. If not improved, permanently discontinue both.
Withhold both for up to 4 weeks. If improved to grade 0-1 or baseline, resume at reduced dose. If not improved, permanently discontinue both.
Consider permanently discontinuing both agents.
Both permanently discontinued.
Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin.

Reduce encorafenib dose to 300mg OD. Encorafenib not recommended in severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C). No dose adjustment for binimetinib in renal impairment. Encorafenib use with caution in severe renal impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: RAS mutation-positive new primary non-cutaneous malignancy
dose_modifications.notes: Consider permanently discontinuing encorafenib (and binimetinib). No dose modification required for new primary cutaneous malignancies.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No specific neutrophil or platelet thresholds stated. Dose modifications based on toxicity grades (NCI-CTC). Serum electrolyte abnormalities (magnesium, potassium) should be corrected before treatment initiation and during treatment. Treatment breaks >6 weeks beyond expected 4-weekly cycle length not permitted without approval.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es every 4 weeks for 24 weeks, then every 12 weeks
Serum electrolytes (magnesium and potassium) — correct abnormalities before and during treatment
LDH every 4 weeks for 24 weeks, then as clinically indicated
Creatine phosphokinase every 4 weeks for 24 weeks, then as indicated
Blood pressure at baseline then every 4 weeks initially, then as indicated
Echo/MUGA at baseline then at 24 weeks unless clinically indicated
QTc interval at baseline, after 4 weeks, then every 12 weeks as indicated
Dermatologic evaluations at baseline then every 8 weeks while on therapy and for up to 24 weeks following discontinuation
Visual assessment at baseline and at each clinic visit
BRAF V600 status confirmed prior to starting treatment
special_warnings: Both binimetinib and encorafenib should be simultaneously interrupted, reduced, or discontinued for most toxicities
Encorafenib 450mg as single agent (without binimetinib) is not well tolerated — reduce to 300mg if binimetinib temporarily interrupted
Avoid concomitant potent CYP3A4 inducers (rifampicin, phenytoin, carbamazepine, St John's wort) or inhibitors with encorafenib
Encorafenib is both a CYP3A4 inhibitor and inducer — caution with CYP3A4 substrates (e.g. hormonal contraceptives)
Encorafenib inhibits UGT1A1 — caution with UGT1A1 substrates (raltegravir, atorvastatin, dolutegravir)
Binimetinib is a potential CYP1A2 inducer — caution with sensitive CYP1A2 substrates (duloxetine, theophylline)
Binimetinib is a weak OAT3 inhibitor — caution with OAT3 substrates (pravastatin, ciprofloxacin)
Binimetinib metabolised through UGT1A1 — caution with UGT1A1 inducers (rifampicin, phenobarbital) and inhibitors (indinavir, atazanavir, sorafenib)
Formal medical review required at least by end of first 8 weeks of treatment
Proton pump inhibitors may reduce efficacy — use H2 antagonist instead where gastric protection needed

required_patient_fields.braf_v600_mutation_status: Must be confirmed positive prior to treatment
required_patient_fields.weight_kg: Not required for dosing (flat dose) but useful for clinical assessment
required_patient_fields.hepatic_function: Child-Pugh class required if hepatic impairment suspected

drugs.special_instructions: Available as 15mg tablets. Swallow whole with water with or without food. If a dose is missed, do not take if less than 6 hours until next dose. In case of vomiting, do not re-take dose — take next scheduled dose. Dose reduction ladder: Level 0 (starting) 45mg BD; Level -1: 30mg BD; Level -2: Not recommended (permanently discontinue).
drugs.notes: Twice daily (days 1-28 of 28-day cycle) | Available as 15mg tablets. Swallow whole with water with or without food. If a dose is missed, do not take if less than 6 hours until next dose. In case of vomiting, do not re-take dose — take next scheduled dose. Dose reduction ladder: Level 0 (starting) 45mg BD; Level -1: 30mg BD; Level -2: Not recommended (permanently discontinue).

drugs.special_instructions: Available as 75mg and 50mg capsules. Swallow whole with water with or without food. If a dose is missed, take only if more than 12 hours until next scheduled dose. In case of vomiting, do not re-take dose. Dose reduction ladder: Level 0 (starting) 450mg OD; Level -1: 300mg OD; Level -2: 200mg OD; Level -3: 100mg OD (limited data; permanently discontinue if unable to tolerate 100mg OD). If binimetinib is temporarily interrupted, reduce encorafenib to 300mg OD during interruption (450mg as monotherapy not well tolerated). Mild hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh A): reduce to 300mg OD. Not recommended in severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C). Use with caution in severe renal impairment.
drugs.notes: Once daily (days 1-28 of 28-day cycle) | Available as 75mg and 50mg capsules. Swallow whole with water with or without food. If a dose is missed, take only if more than 12 hours until next scheduled dose. In case of vomiting, do not re-take dose. Dose reduction ladder: Level 0 (starting) 450mg OD; Level -1: 300mg OD; Level -2: 200mg OD; Level -3: 100mg OD (limited data; permanently discontinue if unable to tolerate 100mg OD). If binimetinib is temporarily interrupted, reduce encorafenib to 300mg OD during interruption (450mg as monotherapy not well tolerated). Mild hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh A): reduce to 300mg OD. Not recommended in severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C). Use with caution in severe renal impairment.

indication: Metastatic or locally advanced cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma in patients who are not candidates for curative surgery or curative radiation.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Every 21 days (continued until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity or up to 24 months, whichever is sooner)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Administer via 0.2-5 micron in-line or add-on filter. Final concentration should be between 1 and 20mg/ml. Patients must be issued with a Patient Alert Card. Do not use systemic corticosteroids or immunosuppressants before starting cemiplimab (except physiological doses ≤10mg/day prednisolone or equivalent). 35 cycles will be set in ARIA.

dose_modifications.condition: Dose reductions not permitted for immunotherapy
dose_modifications.notes: Dose reductions of cemiplimab are NOT permitted. Treatment is either continued, withheld, or permanently discontinued based on toxicity. Funding approval precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer.

dose_modifications.condition: No dose modification required for haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: No requirement for dose modification or treatment interruption due to haematological toxicity. Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin if symptomatic anaemia or Hb <8g/dL (80g/L).
Withhold until symptoms resolve, radiographic abnormalities improve, and management with corticosteroids is complete.
Permanently discontinue cemiplimab.
Withhold until symptoms resolve and management with corticosteroids (if needed) is complete.
Permanently discontinue cemiplimab.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 elevation in AST, ALT, or total bilirubin
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until laboratory values return to baseline and management with corticosteroids (if needed) is complete.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or 4 elevation in AST, ALT, or total bilirubin
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue cemiplimab.
Withhold until creatinine returns to baseline and management with corticosteroids is complete.
Permanently discontinue cemiplimab.

dose_modifications.condition: Symptomatic endocrinopathies (hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, hypophysitis, adrenal insufficiency, diabetes)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until symptoms resolve and management with corticosteroids (if needed for acute inflammation) is complete. Cemiplimab may be continued with hormone replacement therapy as long as no symptoms present. Isolated hypothyroidism can be managed with replacement therapy without interruption.
Withhold dose until symptoms resolve and management with corticosteroids is complete.
Permanently discontinue cemiplimab.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 or 3 immune-related adverse reactions persisting despite treatment modifications or corticosteroid reduction to 10mg prednisolone equivalent cannot be achieved
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue cemiplimab.

dose_modifications.condition: Any recurrence of a severe or NCI-CTC grade 3 reaction; any persistent NCI-CTC grade 2 or 3 treatment-related adverse reaction not recovering to grade 1 within 12 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue cemiplimab.

dose_modifications.condition: Patient still receiving immunosuppressive doses of corticosteroids or other immunosuppressive therapy
dose_modifications.notes: Do not resume cemiplimab while still on immunosuppressive doses.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No haematological delay thresholds. FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle for 8 cycles then every 12 weeks or as clinically indicated. Cemiplimab should be withheld for: NCI-CTC grade 2 pneumonitis or hepatitis; grade 2-3 colitis or renal impairment; any other severe or grade 3 treatment-related adverse reaction. Funding precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle for 8 cycles then every 12 weeks or as clinically indicated
Thyroid function tests at baseline then every 6 weeks or as clinically indicated
Blood glucose monitoring every 6 weeks or as clinically indicated
Patient Alert Card must be issued
Monitor for immune-related adverse reactions: gastrointestinal, hepatic, pulmonary, endocrine, renal, skin, neurological
special_warnings: Cemiplimab is an immunotherapy — dose reductions are NOT permitted; doses are withheld or permanently discontinued based on toxicity
Autoimmune toxicities can be severe and life-threatening — early diagnosis and appropriate management essential
Do not resume cemiplimab if patient still receiving immunosuppressive doses of corticosteroids or other immunosuppressive therapy
Funding approval precludes further treatment after an interruption of 12 weeks or longer
Systemic corticosteroids or immunosuppressants before starting cemiplimab should be avoided (except physiological doses ≤10mg/day prednisolone or equivalent)
Immune-related reactions may occur months after the last dose
No pharmacokinetic drug-drug interaction studies have been conducted with cemiplimab
Treatment up to 24 months (35 cycles in ARIA)

drugs.frequency: Every 21 days (continued until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity or up to 24 months, whichever is sooner)
drugs.special_instructions: Administer via 0.2-5 micron in-line or add-on filter. Final concentration should be between 1 and 20mg/ml. Patients must be issued with a Patient Alert Card. Do not use systemic corticosteroids or immunosuppressants before starting cemiplimab (except physiological doses ≤10mg/day prednisolone or equivalent). 35 cycles will be set in ARIA.
drugs.notes: Every 21 days (continued until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity or up to 24 months, whichever is sooner) | Administer via 0.2-5 micron in-line or add-on filter. Final concentration should be between 1 and 20mg/ml. Patients must be issued with a Patient Alert Card. Do not use systemic corticosteroids or immunosuppressants before starting cemiplimab (except physiological doses ≤10mg/day prednisolone or equivalent). 35 cycles will be set in ARIA.

indication: Ipilimumab in combination with nivolumab is recommended for the treatment of advanced, unresectable metastatic melanoma. WHO performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Every 21 days for 4 cycles (combination phase), then monotherapy maintenance
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Administer FIRST during combination phase. Allow 30 minutes between administration of nivolumab and ipilimumab. Administer via 0.2-1.2 micron low protein binding filter. Nivolumab is neutral on extravasation. Dose banded according to agreed bands. Patient must be given nivolumab Patient Alert Card. After 4 cycles, transition to single-agent nivolumab: 240mg every 14 days (3 weeks after last combination dose) OR 480mg every 28 days (6 weeks after last combination dose).

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Every 21 days for 4 cycles (combination phase only)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Administer SECOND during combination phase (30 minutes after nivolumab). Administer using a low protein binding filter. Final concentration should be between 1-4mg/ml. Ipilimumab is neutral on extravasation. Dose banded according to agreed bands. At cycle 4, consider switching to single agent nivolumab. Avoid systemic corticosteroids before starting ipilimumab. Proton pump inhibitors may reduce efficacy — use H2 antagonist instead.

pre_medications.notes: PRN — not routine premedication. No routine antiemetics required.
PRN — check patient has not already taken paracetamol; maximum 4g per 24 hours; 4 hours between doses

dose_modifications.condition: Dose reductions not permitted for immunotherapy combination
dose_modifications.notes: Dose reductions of ipilimumab or nivolumab are NOT permitted. Treatment is either delayed (both drugs) or permanently discontinued. There is little need to adjust dose for haematological toxicity — dose delay criteria apply regardless of which drug caused the event.
Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin.

dose_modifications.condition: AST/ALT 3-5x ULN and/or total bilirubin 1.5-3x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold both and administer corticosteroids. Upon improvement to NCI-CTC grade 1, taper corticosteroid over 1 month. Combination may be re-started when liver function remains at grade 1 following taper.

dose_modifications.condition: AST/ALT ≥5x ULN or bilirubin >3x ULN; OR hepatic injury not improving to grade 1 within 12 weeks; OR corticosteroid not reducible to ≤10mg prednisolone equivalent within 12 weeks; OR any NCI-CTC grade 3 or above
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue both ipilimumab and nivolumab.

dose_modifications.parameter: Renal impairment — NCI-CTC grade 2 or 3 creatinine elevation
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold both; initiate corticosteroids. Upon improvement to grade 1, initiate taper over at least 1 month. May resume when reaction remains at grade 1 or below following taper.

dose_modifications.parameter: Renal impairment — NCI-CTC grade 4 creatinine elevation; or not improving to grade 1 within 12 weeks
dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 creatinine elevation OR failure to improve to grade 1 within 12 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue both. Initiate corticosteroids at 1-2 mg/kg/day methylprednisolone equivalents for grade 4.

dose_modifications.parameter: Immune-related GI toxicity — NCI-CTC grade 1-2 diarrhoea/mild-moderate colitis
dose_modifications.condition: Grade 1 or 2 diarrhoea / mild to moderate colitis
dose_modifications.notes: May continue on combination with symptomatic treatment and close monitoring. If mild-moderate symptoms recur or persist 5-7 days, omit scheduled doses and consider prednisolone 1mg/kg orally OD. If resolution to grade 0-1 or return to baseline, resume at next scheduled dose. Doses omitted due to adverse reaction must not be replaced.

dose_modifications.parameter: Immune-related GI toxicity — NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 diarrhoea/colitis
dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or 4 diarrhoea, colitis, peritoneal signs, bowel perforation, ileus, fever
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue both. Initiate high-dose IV corticosteroids immediately (unless bowel perforation). Consider infliximab 5mg/kg if symptoms do not respond to steroids in 5-7 days.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 2 neuropathy likely related to ipilimumab or nivolumab
dose_modifications.notes: Omit scheduled dose. If neurologic symptoms resolve to baseline, patient may resume at next scheduled dose.
Permanently discontinue both ipilimumab and nivolumab.
May remain on therapy with symptomatic treatment (topical corticosteroids, antihistamines). For persistent grade 1-2 rash/pruritus consider oral corticosteroids (prednisolone 1mg/kg OD).
Omit scheduled dose. If initial symptoms improve to grade 1 or resolve, therapy may resume at next scheduled dose.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 4 rash or grade 3 pruritus
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue both. Consider systemic corticosteroid therapy.

dose_modifications.condition: Any other immune-related reaction at NCI-CTC grade 3 or above (uveitis, eosinophilia, lipase elevation, glomerulonephritis, iritis, haemolytic anaemia, amylase elevations, multi-organ failure, pneumonitis)
dose_modifications.notes: Consider immediate high-dose corticosteroid therapy and discontinuation of both ipilimumab and nivolumab. For uveitis/iritis/episcleritis: consider topical corticosteroid eyedrops.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No specific neutrophil or platelet thresholds. FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of each cycle. Blood pressure prior to treatment. Thyroid function tests prior to starting and before each administration (cycle) or when clinically indicated. Funding precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer. Both drugs must be delayed until treatment can resume for any drug-related adverse event.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of each cycle
Blood pressure prior to each treatment
Thyroid function tests prior to starting treatment and before each administration (cycle) or when clinically indicated
Monitor for immune-related adverse reactions: GI, hepatic, pulmonary, endocrine, renal, neurological, skin, ocular
Endoscopy if persistent NCI-CTC grade 2 diarrhoea or grade 1-2 diarrhoea with bleeding
Patient must be given nivolumab Patient Alert Card
special_warnings: Ipilimumab and nivolumab are immunotherapies — dose reductions are NOT permitted; only delay or permanent discontinuation
Autoimmune toxicities can be severe and life-threatening
Nivolumab must be administered FIRST; 30 minutes must elapse between both agents
Avoid systemic corticosteroids before starting ipilimumab (may interfere with pharmacodynamic activity); safe to use after starting to treat immune-related adverse reactions
Proton pump inhibitors may reduce efficacy of the combination — consider H2 antagonist for gastric protection instead
Funding approval precludes further treatment after an interruption of 12 weeks or longer
After 4 cycles of combination, transition to single-agent nivolumab 240mg q14 days or 480mg q28 days
At cycle 4, consider switching to single agent nivolumab (ARIA warning included)
Doses omitted due to adverse reaction must not be replaced
Immune-related reactions may occur months after the last dose

required_patient_fields.weight_kg: Required for mg/kg dosing of both ipilimumab and nivolumab (dose banded per agreed bands)

drugs.frequency: Every 21 days for 4 cycles (combination phase), then monotherapy maintenance
drugs.special_instructions: Administer FIRST during combination phase. Allow 30 minutes between administration of nivolumab and ipilimumab. Administer via 0.2-1.2 micron low protein binding filter. Nivolumab is neutral on extravasation. Dose banded according to agreed bands. Patient must be given nivolumab Patient Alert Card. After 4 cycles, transition to single-agent nivolumab: 240mg every 14 days (3 weeks after last combination dose) OR 480mg every 28 days (6 weeks after last combination dose).
drugs.notes: Every 21 days for 4 cycles (combination phase), then monotherapy maintenance | Administer FIRST during combination phase. Allow 30 minutes between administration of nivolumab and ipilimumab. Administer via 0.2-1.2 micron low protein binding filter. Nivolumab is neutral on extravasation. Dose banded according to agreed bands. Patient must be given nivolumab Patient Alert Card. After 4 cycles, transition to single-agent nivolumab: 240mg every 14 days (3 weeks after last combination dose) OR 480mg every 28 days (6 weeks after last combination dose).

drugs.frequency: Every 21 days for 4 cycles (combination phase only)
drugs.special_instructions: Administer SECOND during combination phase (30 minutes after nivolumab). Administer using a low protein binding filter. Final concentration should be between 1-4mg/ml. Ipilimumab is neutral on extravasation. Dose banded according to agreed bands. At cycle 4, consider switching to single agent nivolumab. Avoid systemic corticosteroids before starting ipilimumab. Proton pump inhibitors may reduce efficacy — use H2 antagonist instead.
drugs.notes: Every 21 days for 4 cycles (combination phase only) | Administer SECOND during combination phase (30 minutes after nivolumab). Administer using a low protein binding filter. Final concentration should be between 1-4mg/ml. Ipilimumab is neutral on extravasation. Dose banded according to agreed bands. At cycle 4, consider switching to single agent nivolumab. Avoid systemic corticosteroids before starting ipilimumab. Proton pump inhibitors may reduce efficacy — use H2 antagonist instead.

indication: Bleomycin, by electrochemotherapy, is recommended as an option for patients with melanoma with cutaneous and subcutaneous nodules. It may also be used for cutaneous metastases from other primary tumour after MDT discussion. Performance status 0, 1, 2.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Single treatment (one cycle). 1 cycle set in ARIA.
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Electrochemotherapy context — this protocol covers only the administration of bleomycin in the setting of electrochemotherapy; it does not cover the electrochemotherapy procedure itself. Administer as intravenous bolus over 30-60 seconds (no less than 30 seconds, no more than 60 seconds). After 8 minutes (to allow drug to diffuse into tissues), proceed with administration of required local anaesthetic. Electrochemotherapy then applied. Patient under general anaesthetic — administer hydrocortisone 100mg IV and chlorphenamine 10mg IV immediately before bleomycin to prevent adverse reactions. Bleomycin dose will be rounded to nearest 1,000 international units (up if halfway). Maximum cumulative dose 400,000 international units in patients <60 years; refer to SPC for older patients. Dose is per m² body surface area. 15,000 IU/m².

pre_medications.notes: Patient is under general anaesthetic and cannot report adverse reactions — administer these agents immediately before bleomycin
Patient is under general anaesthetic and cannot report adverse reactions — administer immediately before bleomycin

dose_modifications.notes: INR must be less than 1.5 prior to prescribing. Platelets must be more than 70x10⁹/L prior to prescribing.
Platelets must be more than 70x10⁹/L prior to prescribing.
Reduce dose to 75% of original dose.
Reduce dose to 50% of original dose.
Hepatic impairment increases the risk of lung dysfunction — exercise caution and monitor closely.

Stop bleomycin until pulmonary symptoms can be fully investigated and a diagnosis made. Risk is greater in those >40 years, history of smoking, underlying lung disease, previous mediastinal radiotherapy, poor renal function, or requiring growth factors.
Maximum cumulative dose 400,000 international units in patients <60 years. Refer to SPC for older patients.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: INR must be <1.5. Platelets must be >70x10⁹/L. FBC, LFTs, U&Es and INR must be checked prior to treatment. Respiratory examination required pre-treatment. ECG before treatment depends on location of nodules (less important for limbs, more important for trunk) and patient's pre-morbid condition — highly recommended for manifest cardiac arrhythmia or previous cardiac event. Presence of pacemaker precludes treatment on the anterior chest wall. This is generally a single treatment (one cycle).

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs, U&Es and INR prior to treatment
Respiratory examination pre-treatment
ECG before treatment (especially if nodules on trunk, cardiac history, arrhythmia, or previous cardiac event)
Creatinine clearance assessment for renal dose adjustment
Monitor for pulmonary toxicity (bleomycin-induced pneumonitis)
special_warnings: This protocol covers ONLY the administration of bleomycin in the setting of electrochemotherapy — it does NOT cover the electrochemotherapy procedure itself
Electrochemotherapy combines chemotherapy with application of electric pulses to facilitate drug delivery into tumour cells
Patient is under general anaesthetic — cannot self-report adverse reactions; premedicate with hydrocortisone and chlorphenamine immediately before bleomycin
Presence of pacemaker precludes treatment on the anterior chest wall
Risk of bleomycin-induced pneumonitis is greater in patients >40 years, smokers, underlying lung disease, previous mediastinal radiotherapy, poor renal function, or requiring growth factors
Maximum cumulative bleomycin dose 400,000 IU in patients <60 years (refer to SPC for older patients)
All dose reductions due to adverse reactions should not be re-escalated in subsequent cycles without consultant approval
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped
Bleomycin extravasation risk is neutral
No antiemetics required
Bleomycin electrochemotherapy: single-session procedure under general or local anaesthesia. Repeat sessions may be given at ≥8 week intervals.

required_patient_fields.cumulative_bleomycin_dose_iu: Must track lifetime exposure against 400,000 IU limit
required_patient_fields.age_years: Relevant to cumulative dose limit and pneumonitis risk

drugs.frequency: Single treatment (one cycle). 1 cycle set in ARIA.

Dose is per m² body surface area. 15,000 IU/m².

indication: Nivolumab is recommended, within its marketing authorisation, as an option for previously treated advanced (unresectable or metastatic) melanoma in adults. WHO performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Every 14 days (12 cycles will be set in ARIA)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Flat dose — doses are delayed, not reduced, for toxicity. Administer via 0.2-1.2 micron low protein binding filter (polyethylene lined giving sets used for paclitaxel with 0.22 micron filter are appropriate). Nivolumab is neutral on extravasation. Patient must be given a nivolumab Patient Alert Card. Avoid systemic corticosteroids before starting nivolumab. If switching from 240mg q14 days to 480mg q28 days, first 480mg dose should be administered 2 weeks after last 240mg dose. If used following ipilimumab-nivolumab combination (4 cycles), first 240mg monotherapy dose administered 3 weeks after last combination dose.

pre_medications.notes: PRN — not routine premedication. No routine antiemetics required.

take_home_medicines.frequency: 4mg initially then 2mg after each loose stool when required (max 16mg/24 hours)

dose_modifications.condition: Dose escalation or reduction not recommended
dose_modifications.notes: Nivolumab is a flat dose. Doses are delayed, NOT reduced, for toxicity. Dose escalation or reduction not recommended. Dosing delay or discontinuation may be required based on individual safety and tolerability.
Little need to adjust dose for haematological toxicity. Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin if symptomatic anaemia or Hb <8g/dL (80g/L).
Withhold until symptoms resolve, radiographic abnormalities improve, and management with corticosteroids is complete.
Permanently discontinue nivolumab.
Discontinue nivolumab.
Withhold until symptoms resolve and management with corticosteroids (if needed) is complete.
Permanently discontinue nivolumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 elevation in AST, ALT, or total bilirubin
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until laboratory values return to baseline and management with corticosteroids (if needed) is complete.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or 4 elevation in AST, ALT, or total bilirubin
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue nivolumab.
Permanently discontinue nivolumab in first instance.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic injury not improving to NCI-CTC grade 1 within 12 weeks OR corticosteroid not reducible to ≤10mg prednisolone equivalent within 12 weeks OR any NCI-CTC grade 3 or above reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue nivolumab.
Withhold until creatinine returns to baseline and management with corticosteroids is complete.
Permanently discontinue nivolumab. Initiate corticosteroids at 1-2mg/kg/day methylprednisolone equivalents.

dose_modifications.condition: Creatinine not improving to grade 1 within 12 weeks OR corticosteroid not reducible to ≤10mg prednisolone equivalent within 12 weeks OR recurrent NCI-CTC grade 3 reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue nivolumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Symptomatic endocrinopathies (hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, hypophysitis, adrenal insufficiency, diabetes)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until symptoms resolve and corticosteroid management (if needed for acute inflammation) is complete. Nivolumab may be continued with hormone replacement therapy as long as no symptoms present. Isolated hypothyroidism can be managed with replacement therapy without interruption.
Withhold dose until symptoms resolve and management with corticosteroids is complete.
Permanently discontinue nivolumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 or 3 immune-related adverse reactions persisting despite treatment modifications, or corticosteroid reduction to 10mg prednisolone equivalent cannot be achieved
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue nivolumab.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 pneumonitis or hepatitis; any life-threatening NCI-CTC grade 4 reaction; recurrence of severe or NCI-CTC grade 3 reaction; persistent NCI-CTC grade 2 or 3 reaction not recovering to grade 1 within 12 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue nivolumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Patient still receiving immunosuppressive doses of corticosteroids or other immunosuppressive therapy
dose_modifications.notes: Do not resume nivolumab while still on immunosuppressive doses.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No specific haematological thresholds for delay. FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of each cycle. Thyroid function tests prior to starting treatment and then every 6 weeks or when clinically indicated. Nivolumab should be withheld for: NCI-CTC grade 2 pneumonitis or hepatitis; any grade 2 or 3 colitis or renal impairment; any other severe or grade 3 treatment-related adverse reaction. Funding precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of each cycle
Thyroid function tests prior to starting treatment and then every 6 weeks or when clinically indicated
Monitor for immune-related adverse reactions: GI, hepatic, pulmonary, endocrine, renal, skin, neurological
Patient Alert Card must be issued and confirmed
Repeat chest imaging monthly as clinically indicated for lung toxicity monitoring
special_warnings: Nivolumab is an immunotherapy — dose reductions are NOT permitted; flat dose only; doses are delayed or permanently discontinued for toxicity
Autoimmune toxicities can be severe and life-threatening — early diagnosis and appropriate management essential
Do not resume nivolumab if patient still receiving immunosuppressive doses of corticosteroids or other immunosuppressive therapy
Avoid systemic corticosteroids before starting nivolumab (potential interference with pharmacodynamic activity and efficacy); safe to use after starting to treat immune-related reactions
Funding approval precludes further treatment after an interruption of 12 weeks or longer
Patient must be given a nivolumab Patient Alert Card
Immune-related reactions may occur months after the last dose
Gastric protection: proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed
When switching from 240mg q14 days to 480mg q28 days: first 480mg dose 2 weeks after last 240mg dose
When following ipilimumab-nivolumab combination: first monotherapy dose 3 weeks after last combination dose (if 240mg q14 days selected)

Patient must be given a nivolumab Patient Alert Card. Avoid systemic corticosteroids before starting nivolumab. If switching from 240mg q14 days to 480mg q28 days, first 480mg dose should be administered 2 weeks after last 240mg dose. If used following ipilimumab-nivolumab combination (4 cycles), first 240mg monotherapy dose administered 3 weeks after last combination dose.
